findings.title: Mobile layout issue
findings.description: On mobile view, the 'Get to know Cookiebot CMP' section appears cut off. The three steps are not fully visible.
findings.fix_prompt: Add media query to adjust padding and styling for mobile devices to ensure all elements are visible.

findings.title: Truncated text on mobile
findings.description: Some text, such as in the 'What our customers and partners are saying' section, appears truncated on mobile devices.
findings.fix_prompt: Use responsive typography and adjust text wrapping to ensure all text is fully visible on mobile devices.

findings.title: Google Analytics network errors
findings.description: Multiple POST requests to Google Analytics failed with net::ERR_ABORTED error.
findings.fix_prompt: Investigate and resolve Google Analytics configuration issues to prevent network errors.

findings.title: Missing alt text for images
findings.description: Some images, such as logos and graphics, lack alt text.
findings.fix_prompt: Add descriptive alt text to all images for better accessibility.

raw_status.summary: The website generally appears to be well-designed and functional, but there are some minor layout and content issues on mobile devices, as well as some Google Analytics errors. Overall, the site is professional and usable.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt:
I ran a reliability audit on https://www.cookiebot.com/en/ and found the following issues.
Please fix all of them:

1. [LOW] Mobile layout issue
   Fix: Add media query to adjust padding and styling for mobile devices to ensure all elements are visible.

2. [LOW] Truncated text on mobile
   Fix: Use responsive typography and adjust text wrapping to ensure all text is fully visible on mobile devices.

3. [MEDIUM] Google Analytics network errors
   Fix: Investigate and resolve Google Analytics configuration issues to prevent network errors.

4. [LOW] Missing alt text for images
   Fix: Add descriptive alt text to all images for better accessibility.

After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.

findings.title: Inconsistent button styling
findings.description: Buttons have different styling and sizes throughout the page
findings.fix_prompt: Standardize button styles to have consistent padding, font size, and color scheme

findings.title: Failed network requests
findings.description: Multiple failed network requests to https://r.stripe.com/0 and https://r.stripe.com/b
findings.fix_prompt: Investigate and fix the cause of failed network requests to ensure proper functionality

findings.title: Duplicate navigation links
findings.description: Duplicate navigation links with same text and href
findings.fix_prompt: Remove duplicate navigation links to improve accessibility and user experience

findings.title: Missing alt text for images
findings.description: Images are missing alt text, which can affect screen reader users
findings.fix_prompt: Add descriptive alt text to all images to improve accessibility

raw_status.summary: The page has some layout inconsistencies, functionality issues, and accessibility concerns that need to be addressed. Overall, the page appears to be mostly functional but requires some improvements to provide a better user experience.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt:
I ran a reliability audit on https://stripe.com/ and found the following issues.
Please fix all of them:

1. [LOW] Inconsistent button styling
   Fix: Standardize button styles to have consistent padding, font size, and color scheme

2. [MEDIUM] Failed network requests
   Fix: Investigate and fix the cause of failed network requests to ensure proper functionality

3. [LOW] Duplicate navigation links
   Fix: Remove duplicate navigation links to improve accessibility and user experience

4. [LOW] Missing alt text for images
   Fix: Add descriptive alt text to all images to improve accessibility

After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.

findings.title: Failed network requests
findings.description: Three video assets failed to load due to 'net::ERR_ABORTED'. This may impact page performance and user experience.
findings.fix_prompt: Check the video asset URLs and ensure they are correct and accessible. Consider optimizing or replacing the assets to prevent similar errors.

findings.title: Console error
findings.description: A console error message indicates that the 'Provider's accounts list is empty.' This may not directly impact the user experience but could indicate an issue with the application's functionality.
findings.fix_prompt: Investigate the cause of the 'Provider's accounts list is empty' error and address it to prevent potential issues.

findings.title: Cookie consent banner
findings.description: The cookie consent banner appears on the page, which may be expected behavior. However, ensure that it complies with relevant regulations and does not obstruct the user experience.
findings.fix_prompt: Review and configure the cookie consent banner to ensure compliance and optimal user experience.

raw_status.summary: The Notion website generally appears to be well-designed and functional, but there are some minor issues related to performance and content that should be addressed.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt:
I ran a reliability audit on https://www.notion.so/ and found the following issues.
Please fix all of them:

1. [LOW] Failed network requests
   Fix: Check the video asset URLs and ensure they are correct and accessible. Consider optimizing or replacing the assets to prevent similar errors.

2. [LOW] Console error
   Fix: Investigate the cause of the 'Provider's accounts list is empty' error and address it to prevent potential issues.

3. [LOW] Cookie consent banner
   Fix: Review and configure the cookie consent banner to ensure compliance and optimal user experience.

After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.

Journey context:

name: Vercel
notes: CAPTCHA or human-verification wall active — manual intervention required | CAPTCHA or human-verification wall active — manual intervention required | CAPTCHA or human-verification wall active — manual intervention required

findings.title: Several network requests are failing
findings.description: The page is making several network requests that are failing with a 403 status code. This could impact the functionality of the page.
findings.fix_prompt: Review the Content Security Policy (CSP) directives and adjust them to allow the necessary requests. For example, add 'https://www.vercel-status.com/api/v2/incidents/unresolved.json' to the 'connect-src' directive.

findings.title: Missing alt text for images
findings.description: Some images on the page are missing alt text, which could impact accessibility for users with visual impairments.
findings.fix_prompt: Add alt text to all images on the page. For example, add an 'alt' attribute to the img tag: ''.

findings.title: Layout issues on mobile devices
findings.description: The page layout appears to be broken on mobile devices, with some elements overlapping or not displaying correctly.
findings.fix_prompt: Review the CSS media queries and adjust the layout to ensure that it is responsive and works correctly on mobile devices.

raw_status.summary: The page appears to be mostly functional, but there are some minor issues with network requests and accessibility. The layout also appears to be broken on mobile devices.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt: I ran a reliability audit on https://vercel.com/ and found the following issues. Please fix all of them: 1. [LOW] Several network requests are failing Fix: Review the Content Security Policy (CSP) directives and adjust them to allow the necessary requests. For example, add 'https://www.vercel-status.com/api/v2/incidents/unresolved.json' to the 'connect-src' directive. 2. [LOW] Missing alt text for images Fix: Add alt text to all images on the page. For example, add an 'alt' attribute to the img tag: ''. 3. [MEDIUM] Layout issues on mobile devices Fix: Review the CSS media queries and adjust the layout to ensure that it is responsive and works correctly on mobile devices. After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.
